# /spec

> Draft a feature spec for an integration interface. Spec is feature-scoped (per [ADR-0006](../../../design/adr/0006-doc-scope-domain-vs-feature.md)) — domain-scoped FDD/TDD/blueprint accumulate the feature's content.

## Usage

```
/spec --feature <slug> [--source fresh|alm|handoff] [--project <name>]
```

`--source handoff` reads a prior d365-ce / d365-fo / reporting handoff manifest to pre-fill the spec.

## Inputs

- `agents.yaml`, `project.config.yaml integration.*`
- [templates/spec.template.md](../../templates/spec.template.md)
- Source: RFP / user story / handoff manifest / ALM extract

## Execution flow

1. Validate slug; scaffold feature folder if missing (via `New-Feature.ps1`).
2. Read `project.config.yaml integration.{patterns, runtimes, nfr}`.
3. (On `--source handoff`): read `projects/{p}/_handoffs/{source-agent}-to-integration-{feature}.handoff.json`; carry forward FRs, ACs, NFR targets, and cross-references.
4. Render `spec.template.md` with sections:
   - §1 Business context (current vs future integration topology)
   - §2 Personas (typical: ops engineer / data steward / partner integrator)
   - §3 FRs with ACs; one per interface or pattern instance
   - §4 NFR targets (latency, throughput per [constitution/12-observability-and-nfr.md](../../constitution/12-observability-and-nfr.md))
   - §5 Pattern selection (event-driven vs batch; specific Azure service)
   - §6 Security (auth model, secrets, network)
   - §7 Out of scope
5. `currentStates=[SPEC_DRAFT]`.

## Output

- `projects/{p}/integration/features/{f}/spec.md`
